Association between genomic/clinical data and drug response

analysis

preprocessing [R] -> training and CV model [python] -> evaluating models [R - not replicable with github] -> plots - python code “src/analysis/3.catboost_py.ipynb” - saved trained models can be found in “output/rds/cv_results_all170.pkl”

Loading libraries

association between features and drug response

  • preprocessing features consistently
    • continuous variables
      • “ALT”, “AST”, “creatinine”, “wbcCount”, “plateletCount” (are right skewed): log transformed + z-score
      • other labs + percentage blood cells + age: z-score
      • binary categorical: mutation, cytogenetics, surface antigens, gender
      • multiple categories: FAB, ELN2022, Disease.status: one-
  • only preprocessing right skewed continuous variables, not scaling
sampleinfo_full <- read.csv("data/oshu/clinical_summary.csv") [1:596,]

sampleinfo <- read.csv("data/oshu/sampleinfo_dss.csv")[1:596,] 

cells <- data.frame(sampleinfo_full[,c(54, 56:57,59:61,63:69,71)], row.names = sampleinfo_full$Sample_ID)
log_vars <- c("ALT", "AST", "creatinine", "wbcCount", "plateletCount") # usually right skewed
log_vars <- c(log_vars, "percentBasophilsPB", "percentEosinophilsPB") # rare population and right skewed with high outliers clinical relevance often lies in fold-change or elevation
cells[log_vars] <- log1p(cells[log_vars])

prior <- data.frame(sampleinfo[c(23,24,26,28)], row.names = sampleinfo$Sample_ID)

mutation <- data.frame(sampleinfo[,c(32:55,58:103)], row.names = sampleinfo$Sample_ID)
mutation <- mutation[,colSums(mutation, na.rm = T) >= 10] # removed columns with < 10 non positive

cyto <- read.csv("data/oshu/clinical_cyto.csv", check.names = F)[1:596,] 
rownames(cyto) <- cyto$Sample_ID
cyto <- cyto[sampleinfo$Sample_ID ,6:17] 
cyto <- cyto[,colSums(cyto, na.rm = T) >= 10]

immuno <- read.csv("data/oshu/clinical_immuno.csv")[1:596,]
immuno <- data.frame(immuno[,4:ncol(immuno)], row.names = immuno$Sample_ID) %>%
  mutate_all(.funs = ~ ifelse(.x=="-", F, ifelse(is.na(.x), NA, T)) )
immuno <- immuno[,colSums(immuno, na.rm = T) >= 10] # removed columns with < 10 non positive
## risk, FAB and diagnosis, age, gender
# unknown is removed
df_all <- data.frame(Age = sampleinfo$Age,
                     timepoint = sampleinfo_full$timeOfSampleCollectionRelativeToInclusion,
                     FAB_subtype = sampleinfo$FAB_subtype, 
                     ELN2022 = sampleinfo$ELN2022, 
                     Gender = sampleinfo_full$gender,
                     DvsT = sampleinfo$Disease.status,
                     row.names = sampleinfo$Sample_ID) %>%
  mutate(
    FAB_subtype = ifelse(FAB_subtype=="M0/M1", "M0", FAB_subtype),
    DvsT = case_when(DvsT %in% c("Relapse","Residual") ~ "RR", .default = DvsT),
    isMale = ifelse(Gender=="Male", TRUE, ifelse(Gender=="Female", FALSE, NA))
    ) %>%
  mutate(
    FAB_subtype = factor(FAB_subtype, levels = c("M0", "M1", "M2", "M3", "M4", "M5", "M7")),
    DvsT = factor(DvsT, levels=c("Diagnosis", "RR", "Remission", "Unknown"), ordered = F)
    ) %>%
  select(-Gender)

saving putting all in the same df; exporting to run in python

X_all_categorical <- bind_cols(df_all[3:ncol(df_all)], prior, mutation, immuno, cyto) # 86

X_all_numerical <- bind_cols(cells, df_all[1])# 15

X_all <- cbind(X_all_numerical, X_all_categorical) # 101

combi.m <- data.frame(sampleinfo[,155:324], row.names = sampleinfo$Sample_ID)
combi.m <- combi.m[,colSums(!is.na(combi.m), na.rm = T) >= 20] # range (23-523)
Y_all <- combi.m # 170

df <- cbind(sampleinfo_full[c("Sample_ID", "patientId")], X_all, Y_all)

# df %>% write.csv("output/supp/ML/preprocessed_df.csv", row.names = F)

helper function

  • categorical
    • missing_rate: proportion of NA values ( < 0.5)
    • entropy: diversity of categories ( > 0.5)
    • n_levels: number of unique categories
    • dominant_prop: proportion of the most frequent category ( < 0.95)
  • numerical
    • Missing rate: How much data is missing ( < 0.5)
    • Skewness: Whether the distribution is symmetric or distorted ( < +-2)
    • Outlier proportion: How many values lie far outside the typical range ( < 0.05)
    • Spread: A proxy for entropy—features with very narrow range may be uninformative (not low)
# missing_rate: proportion of NA values
# entropy: diversity of categories
# n_levels: number of unique categories
# dominant_prop: proportion of the most frequent category
score_categorical_features <- function(df, threshold_missing = 0.5, threshold_entropy = 0.5, threshold_dominant_prop = 0.95) {
  
  # Helper: Shannon's entropy function
  calc_entropy <- function(x) {
    freq <- table(x) / length(x)
    -sum(freq * log2(freq + 1e-9))  # add small value to avoid log(0)
  }
  
  # Identify categorical columns (factor or character)
  cat_cols <- names(df)[sapply(df, function(col) is.logical(col) || is.factor(col) || is.character(col))]
  
  # Score each categorical feature
  scores <- lapply(cat_cols, function(col_name) {
    col <- df[[col_name]]
    missing_rate <- mean(is.na(col))
    entropy <- calc_entropy(col[!is.na(col)])
    n_levels <- length(unique(col[!is.na(col)]))
    dominant_prop <- max(table(col[!is.na(col)]) / length(col[!is.na(col)]))
    
    data.frame(
      feature = col_name,
      missing_rate = missing_rate,
      entropy = entropy,
      n_levels = n_levels,
      dominant_prop = dominant_prop,
      keep = (missing_rate < threshold_missing) & (entropy > threshold_entropy) & (dominant_prop < threshold_dominant_prop)
    )
  }) %>% bind_rows()
  
  return(scores)
}

# Missing rate: How much data is missing
# Skewness: Whether the distribution is symmetric or distorted
# Outlier proportion: How many values lie far outside the typical range
# Spread: A proxy for entropy—features with very narrow range may be uninformative
score_numerical_features <- function(df, threshold_missing = 0.5, threshold_skew = 2, threshold_outlier_prop = 0.05) {
  library(dplyr)
  
  # Identify numeric columns
  num_cols <- names(df)[sapply(df, is.numeric)]
  
  # Score each numeric feature
  scores <- lapply(num_cols, function(col_name) {
    col <- df[[col_name]]
    col_clean <- col[!is.na(col)]
    
    missing_rate <- mean(is.na(col))
    skewness <- e1071::skewness(col_clean)
    
    # Outlier detection using 1.5 * IQR rule
    qnt <- quantile(col_clean, probs = c(0.25, 0.75))
    iqr <- IQR(col_clean)
    lower <- qnt[1] - 1.5 * iqr
    upper <- qnt[2] + 1.5 * iqr
    outlier_prop <- mean(col_clean < lower | col_clean > upper)
    
    # Spread proxy: log of range
    spread <- log1p(max(col_clean) - min(col_clean))
    
    keep <- (missing_rate < threshold_missing) & 
            (abs(skewness) < threshold_skew) & 
            (outlier_prop < threshold_outlier_prop)
    
    data.frame(
      feature = col_name,
      missing_rate = missing_rate,
      skewness = skewness,
      outlier_prop = outlier_prop,
      spread = spread,
      keep = keep
    )
  }) %>% bind_rows()
  
  return(scores)
}

score_categorical_features(X_all_categorical) %>% DT::datatable()
score_numerical_features(X_all_numerical) %>% DT::datatable()

running catboost + shap in python

  • refer to src/analysis/3.catboost_py.ipynb

  • SHAP values are in the same units as the model output (e.g., DSS score)

    • global SHAP values for continuous variables and categorical variables should be looked at separately
    • comparing Global SHAP (e.g., mean absolute SHAP per feature) summarizes importance across the dataset

  • running with default model

    • 170 combinations
    • 101 features - 15 numerical, 86 categorical
    • using grouped 5-fold CV
    • evaluate the performance of the model R^2 or RMSE to ensure that there isnt overfitting/underfitting -> affects the reliability of the SHAP values

  • advantage

    • native handling of categorical features && missingness && its default model is quite balanced in bias-variance (but non-random missingness still affects); stability with small datasets

  • to consider

    • not well tuned models (over/underfitting); black box
    • doesnt capture interactions fully
    • small sample size / no test set/ noise causing overestimating generalizability
    • cross drug/model compatibility

  • what is exported

    • per combination
      • fold-wise shap values on test sets
      • stability metrics of each feature
      • sample level + fold-wise residuals (obs.val - pred.val)
      • sample level + fold-wise shap values

[not included in github] evaluation

  • obtain raw files by running the saved python models
    • “_shap_foldwise.csv”; “_residuals.csv”
base.dir = "output/supp/ML/diagnostics/"
listofcombi <- read.csv("data/oshu/combi_detail.csv")[,c(1,2,4,5)] %>% unique() %>%
  mutate(changedname = gsub("[ -]", ".", combo))

topcombi <- read.csv("output/oshu_combinationindex.csv") %>% 
  mutate(combo = Combination,
         Combination = gsub("[ -]", ".", Combination))

r_squared <- function(y_val, y_pred) {
  ss_res <- sum((y_val - y_pred)^2)
  ss_tot <- sum((y_val - mean(y_val))^2)
  1 - (ss_res / ss_tot)
}

df <- read.csv("output/supp/ML/preprocessed_df.csv", check.names = F) %>%
  rename(DiseaseStatus = DvsT)
X_all <- df[3:103]
categories <- data.frame(feature = colnames(X_all),
                         type = c(rep("numeric", 15), rep("category", 86)))

tbl8: model evaluation

  • diagnosing the cause of instability
    • small sample size
    • missingness of information / noise/ outlier
    • poor signals - lack of strong signal from a single feature
    • the target is just all max/min out
  • filtering out unstable models
    • kendall tau - directly compares pairs of data points to see if they are concordant (spearman reports pearson’s of the ranks)
      • important to also eval the p values - such that the coef isnt just randomness
      • using 1-sided test “greater” to only look for positive corr; rsq > 0 to ensure that its better than mean
  • stability determined by the no. of positive and signif tau values
    • poor: 0-1: model is unable to consistently find a signal in the data that generalizes across different subsets.
    • unstable: 2-3: tentative but inconsistent signal
    • stable: 4-5
rawfiles = list.files(path = base.dir, pattern = ".*_residuals.csv")
rawfiles_name <- gsub("_residuals.csv", "", rawfiles)

df_list <- lapply(rawfiles, function(i) read.csv(paste0(base.dir, i)))
names(df_list) <- rawfiles_name

perf_summary_df <- rbindlist(df_list, idcol = "changedname")

perf_summary_perfold <- perf_summary_df %>%
  group_by(changedname, fold) %>%
  summarise(
    PearsonR = round(cor(y_true, y_pred), 5),
    PearsonP = signif(cor.test(y_true, y_pred, method = "pearson")$p.value, 5),
    KendallT = round(cor(y_true, y_pred, method = "kendall"), 5),
    KendallP = signif(cor.test(y_true, y_pred, method = "kendall", exact = T, continuity = T, alternative = "greater")$p.value, 5),
    rsq = round(r_squared(y_true, y_pred), 5),
    n_samples_perfold = n()) %>% ungroup() %>%
  mutate(sig_kendall = KendallP < 0.05 & rsq > 0) %>%
  group_by(changedname) %>%
  mutate(
    n_samples = sum(n_samples_perfold),
    n_sig_fold = sum(sig_kendall),
    category = case_when( 
      n_sig_fold >= 4 ~ "stable", 
      n_sig_fold >= 2 ~ "unstable",
      .default = "poor"
      )
    ) %>% ungroup()  %>%
  left_join(topcombi[,c(1,5,11,12)], by=c("changedname" = "Combination")) %>%
  dplyr::select(Combination := last_col(), everything()) %>% dplyr::select(-2)

## table 8
write.csv(perf_summary_perfold, "output/model_perf_summary_perfold.csv", row.names = F)

perf_summary <- perf_summary_perfold %>%
  select(Combination, n_samples, n_sig_fold, category, median_CI, Better_combi) %>%
  distinct_all() 

table(perf_summary$category)
cut(perf_summary$n_samples, breaks = seq(0, 530, by=25), include.lowest = TRUE) %>% table(., perf_summary$category)

write.csv(perf_summary, "output/supp/ML/model_perf_summary_df.csv", row.names = F)

feature stability (gathering SHAP values)

  • Using SHAP values from the test folds of a 5-fold CV is a statistically sound and biologically compelling method for your goal. This is a powerful, defensible, and rigorous approach that directly addresses the core question of robustness.

  • entropy < 1.5

    • Entropy ranges from 0 (perfectly stable rank) to log(N) where N is number of ranks (e.g. log₂(5) ≈ 2.32 for 5 folds).

  • cv < 0.5

    • = SD / mean of absolute SHAP values (changing SD to R’s instead of np.std)
    • implies that the SHAP values vary less than half their mean—reasonable for biological interpretation.

  • high mean + low sd == low CV

        Better_combi

    category FALSE TRUE poor 71 56 stable 4 13 unstable 9 17

rawfiles = list.files(path = base.dir, pattern = ".*_feature_stability.csv")
rawfiles_name <- gsub("_feature_stability.csv", "", rawfiles)

df_list <- lapply(rawfiles, function(i) read.csv(paste0(base.dir, i)))
names(df_list) <- rawfiles_name

rawfiles_1 = list.files(path = base.dir, pattern = ".*_shap_foldwise.csv")

feature_summary_df <- rbindlist(df_list, idcol = "changedname") %>%
  mutate(feature = ifelse(feature == "DvsT", "DiseaseStatus", feature))

feature_summary_df <- feature_summary_df %>%
  left_join(topcombi[,c(1,12)], by=c("changedname" = "Combination")) %>%
  dplyr::select(Combination := last_col(), everything()) %>% dplyr::select(-2) %>%
  left_join(categories) %>%
  group_by(Combination, type) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_abs_shap, ties.method = "average"),
         rank = case_when(mean_abs_shap == 0 & type == "numeric" ~ 15,
                          mean_abs_shap == 0 & type == "category" ~ 86,
                          .default = rank) # when feature has no contribution
         ) 

write.csv(feature_summary_df, "output/supp/ML/feature_summary_df.csv", row.names = F)

feature stability of reliable folds and models only

  • only considering (poor models are not included)

    • all folds of stable models

    • sig folds of unstable models

      poor stable unstable 127 17 26

0 1 2 3 4 5 92 35 13 13 11 6

na_mask <- perf_summary_perfold %>%
  mutate(fold = paste0("fold", fold) ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Combination, fold, sig_kendall, n_samples, n_sig_fold, category, median_CI, Better_combi) %>%
  filter(category != "poor")

x <- feature_summary_df[,c(1,2,7:11, 17)] %>% ungroup() %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("fold"), names_to = "fold", values_to = "mean_abs_shap") %>% 
  mutate(fold = gsub("_mean_abs_shap", "", fold)) %>%
  inner_join(na_mask) 

## keeping only sig folds
unstable_feature_sum <- x %>% filter(category == "unstable", sig_kendall == T) %>%
  group_by(Combination, feature, category, median_CI, Better_combi, n_sig_fold, n_samples, type) %>%
  summarise(mean_abs_shap = mean(mean_abs_shap))  %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Combination, type) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_abs_shap, ties.method = "average"),
         rank = case_when(mean_abs_shap == 0 & type == "numeric" ~ 15,
                          mean_abs_shap == 0 & type == "category" ~ 86,
                          .default = rank) # when feature has no contribution
         ) 
write.csv(unstable_feature_sum, "output/supp/ML/unstable_feature_summary_df.csv", row.names = F)

## using all folds for robustness
stable_feature_sum <- x %>% filter(category == "stable") %>%
  group_by(Combination, feature, category, median_CI, Better_combi, n_sig_fold, n_samples, type) %>%
  summarise(mean_abs_shap = mean(mean_abs_shap))  %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Combination, type) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_abs_shap, ties.method = "average"),
         rank = case_when(mean_abs_shap == 0 & type == "numeric" ~ 15,
                          mean_abs_shap == 0 & type == "category" ~ 86,
                          .default = rank) # when feature has no contribution
         ) 
write.csv(stable_feature_sum, "output/supp/ML/stable_feature_summary_df.csv", row.names = F)

tbl9: saving all summary

  • saving per feature per model averaged across selected folds
  • saving poor models at the bottom
perf_summary <- read.csv("output/supp/ML/model_perf_summary_df.csv") %>% filter(category =="poor")
stable_feature_sum <- read.csv("output/supp/ML/stable_feature_summary_df.csv")
unstable_feature_sum <- read.csv("output/supp/ML/unstable_feature_summary_df.csv")
df <- rbind(stable_feature_sum, unstable_feature_sum) %>%
  arrange(category, Combination, rank) %>%
  bind_rows(perf_summary) 

write.csv(df, "output/all_feature_summary_df.csv", row.names = F)

tbl 10b: shap vs feature value

  • for beeswarm plot
  • combining
    • for each combination
    • for each sample
    • for each feature
      • test shap value
      • feature value
      • observed DSS
## shap per sample
rawfiles = list.files(path = base.dir, pattern = ".*_shap_stacked_sample_by_feature.csv")
rawfiles_name <- gsub("_shap_stacked_sample_by_feature.csv", "", rawfiles)

df_list <- lapply(rawfiles, function(i) read.csv(paste0(base.dir, i), check.names = F))
names(df_list) <- rawfiles_name

shap_per_sample_df <- rbindlist(df_list, idcol = "changedname") %>%
  rename(DiseaseStatus = DvsT)

## outputs per sample
rawfiles = list.files(path = base.dir, pattern = ".*_residuals.csv")
rawfiles_name <- gsub("_residuals.csv", "", rawfiles)

df_list <- lapply(rawfiles, function(i) read.csv(paste0(base.dir, i), check.names = F))
names(df_list) <- rawfiles_name

DSS_per_sample_df <- rbindlist(df_list, idcol = "changedname") 

## combine
shap_summary_df <- bind_cols(DSS_per_sample_df, shap_per_sample_df[, 2:102]) %>%
  mutate(Combination = listofnames[changedname]) %>%
  select(Combination, everything())

write.csv(shap_summary_df, "output/shap_summary_df.csv", row.names = F)

plot

perf_summary <- read.csv("output/ML/model_perf_summary_df.csv")
feature_summary_df <- read.csv("output/ML/feature_summary_df.csv")
shap_summary_df <- read.csv("output/ML/shap_summary_df.csv", check.names = F)

stable_feature_sum <- read.csv("output/ML/stable_feature_summary_df.csv")
unstable_feature_sum <- read.csv("output/ML/unstable_feature_summary_df.csv")

df <- read.csv("output/ML/preprocessed_df.csv", check.names = F) %>%
  rename(DiseaseStatus = DvsT)
X_all <- df[3:103]
rownames(X_all) <- df$Sample_ID
Y_all <- df[104:273]
rownames(Y_all) <- df$Sample_ID

fig 4d: heatmap of stable + unstable shap (n=43)

categories <- data.frame(feature = colnames(X_all),
                         type = c(rep("numeric", 15), rep("category", 86))) 

df <- rbind(stable_feature_sum, unstable_feature_sum)

## matrix of rank for heatmap
df_wide <- reshape2::acast(df, Combination ~ feature, value.var = "rank", fill = 0) 

df_wide_cat <- df_wide[, categories$feature[categories$type == "category"] ]
df_wide_cat <- df_wide_cat[, order(colMedians(df_wide_cat), decreasing = F)]

df_wide_num <- df_wide[, categories$feature[categories$type == "numeric"] ]
df_wide_num <- df_wide_num[, order(colMedians(df_wide_num), decreasing = F)]

## split between unstable and stable
split <- distinct_all(df[,c(1,3)]) %>% column_to_rownames("Combination")
split <- split[rownames(df_wide),]

col_fun1 <- colorRamp2(c(86, 10, 1), c("#f7fbff", "#6baed6", "#08306b"))
col_fun2 <- colorRamp2(c(15, 5, 1), c("#f7fcf5", "#74c476", "#00441b"))

## barplot annot
ha1 = HeatmapAnnotation(RankDistribtion = anno_boxplot(df_wide_cat, height = unit(3, "cm"), gp = gpar(fill = "#6baed6"), axis_param = list(side = "right", direction = "reverse")), annotation_name_rot = 0)
ha2 = HeatmapAnnotation(RankDistribtion = anno_boxplot(df_wide_num, height = unit(3, "cm"), gp = gpar(fill = "#74c476"), axis_param = list(direction = "reverse" )), show_annotation_name = F, annotation_name_rot = 0)

## heatemap
hm1 <- Heatmap(df_wide_cat, name = "Category SHAP Rank", 
        rect_gp = gpar(col = "white", lwd=0.5),
        col = col_fun1, cluster_columns = F, #cluster_rows = F,
        clustering_distance_rows = "pearson",
        show_row_dend = F,
        bottom_annotation = ha1,
        column_title = "category",
        width = unit(0.15*ncol(df_wide_cat), "in"),
        height = unit(0.15*nrow(df_wide_cat), "in"),
        row_split = split
        # right_annotation = row_anno
        )

hm2 <- Heatmap(df_wide_num, name = "Numeric SHAP Rank", 
        rect_gp = gpar(col = "white", lwd=0.5),
        col = col_fun2, cluster_columns = F, #cluster_rows = F,
        clustering_distance_rows = "pearson",
        show_row_dend = F,
        bottom_annotation = ha2,
        column_title = "numeric",
        width = unit(0.15*ncol(df_wide_num), "in"),
        height = unit(0.15*nrow(df_wide_num), "in"),
        row_split = split
        )

png("plot/fig4/main_4d_catboost_hm.png", res=300, width = 0.15*ncol(df_wide) + 5, height = 0.15*nrow(df_wide) + 5, units = "in")
Warning: unable to open file 'plot/fig4/main_4d_catboost_hm.png' for writingWarning: opening device failedError in png("plot/fig4/main_4d_catboost_hm.png", res = 300, width = 0.15 *  : 
  unable to start png() device

tbl10a & fig 4c: scatterplot across models

df <- rbind(stable_feature_sum, unstable_feature_sum) %>%
  filter(mean_abs_shap != 0, # filtering out uninformative features
         ) %>% 
  mutate(norm_rank = case_when(type == "numeric" ~ rank/15, type == "category" ~ rank/86) 
         ) %>%
  group_by(feature, type) %>%
  reframe(mean_rank = mean(norm_rank),
          median_rank = median(norm_rank),
         sd_rank = sd(norm_rank),
         n_model = n()
         )

annotating_features <- cbind(categories,
                             category = c(
                               rep("clinical labs", 14),
                               rep("patient info", 9), 
                               rep("mutation", 42),
                               rep("IHC", 28),
                               rep("cytogenetics", 8)
                             )
                             )

df <- left_join(df, annotating_features)
Joining with `by = join_by(feature, type)`
write.csv(df, "output/ML/top_feature_summary_df.csv", row.names = F)

df %>%
  ggplot(., aes(x =mean_rank, y = sd_rank, label = feature, size = n_model, color = category, shape = type)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(size = 3, max.overlaps = 17) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#377EB8", "#4DAF4A", "#E6AB02",  "#A65628", "#F781BF")) +
  # scale_size(range = c(1,8)) +
  scale_radius(range = c(0.1,3)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, lty=2) +
  scale_y_continuous(transform = "log10") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.25, lty=2)  +
  theme_minimal(base_family = 14) +
  labs(#title = "SHAP Feature Rank Stability Across Models",
       x = "Mean Rank (lower = more important)",
       y = "Rank Standard Deviation (lower = more stable)") +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(label="", size=3) ),
         shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=3) )) 

ggsave("plot/fig4/main_4c_global_shap.png", width=6.5, height=7)

observation (using stable & unstable only)

## to use both stable and unstable
perf_summary_perfold <- read.csv("output/ML/model_perf_summary_perfold.csv")
keep_fold <- perf_summary_perfold %>% filter(category == "stable"|(category == "unstable" & sig_kendall == T)) 

all_feature_sum <- rbind(stable_feature_sum, unstable_feature_sum)
shap_summary_df_filt <- inner_join(shap_summary_df, keep_fold[1:2])
Joining with `by = join_by(Combination, fold)`
# write.csv(shap_summary_df_filt, "output/ML/shap_summary_df_filt.csv", row.names = F)

selected

# setting defaults
color_category <- c(subtype.col3, eln.col[1:4], disease.col3, 
                    "RR" = "firebrick",
                    "TRUE" = "#c5247d",
                    "FALSE" = "#4d9220")
# x_range = shap_values$shap_value %>% quantile(probs = c(0,1))
type = ""

color_setting <- if(type == "category"){ 
  scale_color_manual(values = color_category, name = "Category\nFeature values")} else {scale_color_distiller(palette = "PiYG", name = "Numeric\nFeature values")}

legend_setting <- if(type == "category"){ 
  theme(legend.key.height = unit(0.1,"cm"))} else {theme(legend.key.height = unit(0.3,"cm"))}
plot_selected_feature <- function(selected, type = "category", rank_ = 3, keep_ = 1){
  
  ## def
  color_category <- c(subtype.col3, eln.col[1:4], disease.col3, 
                      "RR" = "firebrick",
                      "TRUE" = "#c5247d",
                      "FALSE" = "#4d9220")
  
  color_setting <- if(type == "category"){ 
    scale_color_manual(values = color_category, name = "Category\nFeature values")} else {scale_color_distiller(palette = "PiYG", name = "Numeric\nFeature values")}
  
  legend_setting <- if(type == "category"){ 
    theme(legend.key.height = unit(0.1,"cm"))} else {theme(legend.key.height = unit(0.3,"cm"))}
  
  ## load
  selected_feature <- all_feature_sum %>%
    filter(feature %in% selected , median_CI <= 1) %>%
    group_by(Combination) %>%
    mutate(keep = sum(rank <= rank_)) %>%
    filter(keep >= keep_ )
    
  shap_values <- shap_summary_df_filt %>%
    filter(Combination %in% selected_feature$Combination) %>%
    select(Combination, sample_index, all_of(selected)) %>%
    reshape2::melt(., id.vars = c("Combination", "sample_index"), variable.name = "feature", value.name = "shap_value")
  
  
  if (type == "category") {
    feature_values <- X_all %>%
      rownames_to_column("sample_index") %>%
      select(sample_index, all_of(selected)) %>%
      reshape2::melt(., id.vars = "sample_index", variable.name = "feature", value.name = "feature_value")
  } else {
    feature_values <- X_all %>%
      rownames_to_column("sample_index") %>%
      select(sample_index, all_of(selected)) %>%
      mutate_at(all_of(selected), .funs = ~minmax_scale(.x)) %>%
      reshape2::melt(., id.vars = "sample_index", variable.name = "feature", value.name = "feature_value")
  }
  
  df <- inner_join(feature_values, shap_values) %>%
    left_join(selected_feature[,c(1,2,10,11)]) %>%
    arrange(keep) %>%
    mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = selected) 
           )
  df$Combination <- factor(df$Combination, levels = pull(distinct(df, Combination)))
  
  if (type == "category") { df <- df %>% mutate(feature_value = factor(feature_value, levels = names(color_category))) }
  
  df %>% 
    ggplot(., aes(x=shap_value, y=Combination, color=feature_value)) +
    ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(cex = 2.5, corral = "wrap", corral.width = 0.7, shape=16, alpha=0.8, size = 1) +
    color_setting +
    labs(x="SHAP values\n \u2190 Resistant                      Sensitive \u2192", y="") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype=6) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
    facet_grid(~feature, scales = "free_x")  +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(colour = "black", size = 11, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black", size = 11),
          panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.25),
          aspect.ratio = 3
          ) +
    legend_setting + 
    if (type == "category") guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=3) ) )
}

plot_selected_feature(selected, type = "category", rank_ = 3, keep_ = 1)

supp fig 5

plot_selected_feature_long <- function(
    selected, type = "category", 
    rank_ = 15){
  
  ## def
  color_category <- c(subtype.col3, eln.col[1:4], disease.col3, 
                      "RR" = "firebrick",
                      "TRUE" = "#c5247d",
                      "FALSE" = "#4d9220")
  
  color_setting <- if(type == "category"){ 
    scale_color_manual(values = color_category, name = "Category\nFeature values")} else {scale_color_distiller(palette = "PiYG", name = "Numeric\nFeature values")}
  
  legend_setting <- if(type == "category"){ 
    theme(legend.key.height = unit(0.1,"cm"))} else {theme(legend.key.height = unit(0.3,"cm"))}
  
  ## load
  selected_feature <- all_feature_sum %>%
    filter(feature %in% selected , median_CI <= 1, rank <= rank_) %>%
    mutate(label = paste0(Combination, " [", rank, "]")) %>%
    arrange(-rank)
    
  shap_values <- shap_summary_df_filt %>%
    filter(Combination %in% selected_feature$Combination) %>%
    select(Combination, sample_index, all_of(selected)) %>%
    reshape2::melt(., id.vars = c("Combination", "sample_index"), variable.name = "feature", value.name = "shap_value")
  
  
  if (type == "category") {
    feature_values <- X_all %>%
      rownames_to_column("sample_index") %>%
      select(sample_index, all_of(selected)) %>%
      reshape2::melt(., id.vars = "sample_index", variable.name = "feature", value.name = "feature_value")
  } else {
    feature_values <- X_all %>%
      rownames_to_column("sample_index") %>%
      select(sample_index, all_of(selected)) %>%
      mutate_at(all_of(selected), .funs = ~minmax_scale(.x)) %>%
      reshape2::melt(., id.vars = "sample_index", variable.name = "feature", value.name = "feature_value")
  }
  
  df <- inner_join(feature_values, shap_values) %>%
    inner_join(selected_feature[,c(1,2,10,11)]) %>%
    mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = selected) ,
         label = factor(label, selected_feature$label))

  if (type == "category") { df <- df %>% mutate(feature_value = factor(feature_value, levels = names(color_category))) }
  
  df %>% 
    ggplot(., aes(x=shap_value, y=label, color=feature_value)) +
    ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(cex = 2.5, corral = "wrap", corral.width = 0.7, shape=16, alpha=0.8, size = 1) +
    color_setting +
    labs(x="SHAP values\n \u2190 Resistant                      Sensitive \u2192", y="") +
    theme_classic(base_size = 11) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype=6) +
    facet_col( ~ feature , drop = T, scale = "free_y", space = "free" )  + 
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(colour = "black", size = 11, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black", size = 11),
          panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.5),
          strip.text = element_text(size = 8)
          ) +
    legend_setting + 
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=3) ) )
}
## "cyto"
selected = c("CBFB-MYH11", "KMT2A_re", "MECOM_re", "PML-RARA", "RUNX1-RUNX1T1", "Other_re", "Other_abn", "Other_abn2")
plot_selected_feature_long(selected, type = "category")

# height = 10

fig 5a: heatmap for schematics

normalized_df <- X_all %>%
  # Normalize numeric columns 1:14
  mutate(across(1:14, ~ if (is.numeric(.)) {
    rng <- range(., na.rm = TRUE)
    (. - rng[1]) / (rng[2] - rng[1])
  } else {
    .
  })) %>%
  
  # Normalize Age separately (if needed)
  mutate(Age = Age / 88) %>%
  
  # Binary encode annotation/logical columns 15:19
  mutate(across(FAB_subtype:Other_abn2, ~ case_when(
    . == TRUE ~ 1L,
    . == FALSE ~ 0L,
    is.na(.) ~ NA_integer_,
    TRUE ~ 1L
  )))

ht_opt(heatmap_row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 16),
       heatmap_column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20))

hm_x1 <- Heatmap(t(normalized_df[1:14]), rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd=0.0001),
                col = colorRamp2(c(0, 1), c("#f7fbff", "#377EB8")),
                cluster_rows = F, cluster_columns = F,
                show_row_names = F, show_column_names = F, show_heatmap_legend = F,
                row_title = "Clinical labs\n(n=14)", row_title_rot = 0,
                column_title = "Samples (n=596)",
                width = unit(6, "in"), height = unit(0.05* 14, "in")
                )

hm_x2 <- Heatmap(t(normalized_df[15:23]), rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd=0.0001),
                col = colorRamp2(c(0, 1), c("#f7fbff", "#F781BF")),
                cluster_rows = F, cluster_columns = F,
                show_row_names = F, show_column_names = F, show_heatmap_legend = F,
                row_title = "Patient info\n(n=9)", row_title_rot = 0,
                width = unit(6, "in"), height = unit(0.05* 9, "in")
                )

hm_x3 <- Heatmap(t(normalized_df[24:65]), rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd=0.0001),
                col = colorRamp2(c(0, 1), c("#f7fbff", "#A65628")),
                cluster_rows = F, cluster_columns = F,
                show_row_names = F, show_column_names = F, show_heatmap_legend = F,
                row_title = "Mutation\n(n=42)", row_title_rot = 0,
                width = unit(6, "in"), height = unit(0.05* 42, "in")
                )

hm_x4 <- Heatmap(t(normalized_df[66:93]), rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd=0.0001),
                col = colorRamp2(c(0, 1), c("#f7fbff", "#E6AB02")),
                cluster_rows = F, cluster_columns = F,
                show_row_names = F, show_column_names = F, show_heatmap_legend = F,
                row_title = "IHC\n(n=28)", row_title_rot = 0,
                width = unit(6, "in"), height = unit(0.05* 28, "in")
                )

hm_x5 <- Heatmap(t(normalized_df[94:101]), rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd=0.0001),
                col = colorRamp2(c(0, 1), c("#f7fbff", "#4DAF4A")),
                cluster_rows = F, cluster_columns = F,
                show_row_names = F, show_column_names = F, show_heatmap_legend = F,
                row_title = "Cytogenetics\n(n=8)", row_title_rot = 0,
                width = unit(6, "in"), height = unit(0.05* 8, "in")
                )

hm_y <- Heatmap(t(Y_all), rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd=0.0001),
                col= colorRamp2(seq(0,1,by=0.1), viridis::viridis(n=11)),
                cluster_rows = F, cluster_columns = F,
                show_row_names = F, show_column_names = F, show_heatmap_legend = F,
                row_title = "Combination\n(n=170)", row_title_rot = 0,
                width = unit(6, "in"),
                height = unit(0.02* 170, "in")
                )

ht_list = hm_x1 %v% hm_x2 %v% hm_x3 %v% hm_x4 %v% hm_x5  

png("plot/catboost_schematic_feature.png", res=1000, width = 8, height = 8, units = "in")
draw(ht_list, row_title = "Features (n=101)", row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20))
dev.off()
null device 
          1 
png("plot/catboost_schematic_y.png", res=1000, width = 8, height = 4, units = "in")
draw(hm_y, row_title = "Targets", row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20))


ht_opt(RESET = TRUE)

output <- .run.cor(X_all_numerical, Y_all, min.n=5, method="spearman", p.adj.m="fdr") %>%
  mutate(Combination = listofnames[Combination])
write.csv(output, "output/spear_numeric_combi.csv", row.names = F)
